pr.title: Add description, variable names, and enums to some GX functions
pr.author: DRGN-DRC

summary: Documentation and readability pass over several Dolphin GX vertex-attribute APIs. The PR replaced anonymous argN parameters and hardcoded switch cases with GX enum-typed names, added Doxygen comments describing vertex descriptors, VAT formats, arrays, vertex-cache invalidation, and texture-gen counts, and enabled JAVADOC_AUTOBRIEF in Doxyfile so those comments are picked up cleanly. It was mainly a semantic/naming cleanup, not a matching-focused rewrite.
what_changed: Enabled Doxygen JAVADOC_AUTOBRIEF by changing Doxyfile from JAVADOC_AUTOBRIEF = NO to YES.
Included <dolphin/gx/GXEnum.h> from GXAttr.c and GXAttr.h so GX API prototypes could use enum types directly.

Renamed GXSetVtxDesc parameters from s32 arg0, s32 arg1 to GXAttr attr, s32 type and replaced numeric switch cases with GX_VA_* constants such as GX_VA_PNMTXIDX, GX_VA_POS, GX_VA_NRM, GX_VA_NBT, GX_VA_CLR0, and GX_VA_TEX0 through GX_VA_TEX7.

Added a Doxygen description for GXSetVtxDesc explaining current vertex descriptors, GP command stream parsing, ascending attribute order, and accepted reference types GX_NONE, GX_DIRECT, GX_INDEX8, and GX_INDEX16.
Renamed GXSetVtxAttrFmt parameters to size_t vtxfmt, GXAttr attr, enum_t count, GXAttrType type, and u8 frac, and updated all internal argN references accordingly.
Replaced numeric GXSetVtxAttrFmt attribute cases with GX_VA_POS, GX_VA_NRM, GX_VA_NBT, GX_VA_CLR0, GX_VA_CLR1, and GX_VA_TEX0 through GX_VA_TEX7 while preserving the INSERT_FIELD bit positions.

Added a long Doxygen description for GXSetVtxAttrFmt covering the Vertex Attribute Format Table, count/type/frac semantics, GXBegin interaction, normal/NBT behavior, and a HW1 fixed-point fraction note.
Renamed GXSetArray parameters to GXAttr attr, s32 base_ptr, and u8 stride; replaced the special-case literal 0x19/0xA mapping with GX_VA_NBT mapped to GX_VA_NRM.

Added a Doxygen description for GXSetArray covering base pointer, stride, indexed attribute address calculation, vertex cache behavior, 32-byte alignment advice, and related matrix/light indexed data functions.
Added a Doxygen description for GXInvalidateVtxCache explaining its use by GXInit and when modifying or relocating data read by the vertex cache.
Renamed GXSetNumTexGens parameter from arg0 to num and added Doxygen explaining generated texture coordinates, TEV use, GX_TEXCOORD0 sequence, max count 8, and the GXSetNumChans relation.
Updated GXAttr.h prototypes to expose the new parameter names and enum-typed signatures for GXSetArray, GXSetVtxAttrFmt, and GXSetVtxDesc.
Changed anonymous typedef enum declarations in GXEnum.h to tagged enum declarations typedef enum GXAttr and typedef enum GXAttrType.
Removed the non-MWERKS_GEKKO NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallback body for __GXSetVCD in the shown diff; there was no review discussion explaining this cleanup.
smart_moves: Used existing GX_VA_* and GX_* enum names to document hardware register programming without changing the underlying INSERT_FIELD layout.
Kept the low-level bitfield operations intact while making the API-level intent visible through typed parameters and switch labels.
Tagged GXAttr and GXAttrType enum declarations so function prototypes can name the enum types consistently.
Documented behavior at the public GX API boundary rather than only renaming locals, making the recovered code more useful to future readers and generated docs.
Preserved important hardware-specific caveats in comments, including normal/NBT sharing, indexed vertex-cache behavior, 32-byte alignment, and the HW1 fraction bug note.
decomp_lessons: When numeric switch cases correspond to known SDK enum values, replacing literals with enum labels can greatly improve readability while leaving matching-sensitive code structure unchanged.
Public decomp headers benefit from semantic parameter names even when internal struct fields remain partially unknown, such as __GXContexts.main->x14, x18, x41C, x41D, and x4F0_flags.
For SDK-like libraries, Doxygen comments can encode recovered API behavior, valid enum domains, and hardware notes without needing to rename every internal context field first.

If a function accepts different enum domains depending on another parameter, documenting that relationship may be safer than forcing an overly narrow type; GXSetVtxAttrFmt uses enum_t count because count may refer to GXCompCnt, GXPosCompCnt, GXNrmCompCnt, GXClrCompCnt, or GXTexCompCnt.
Use named constants for special attribute aliases, such as treating GX_VA_NBT as GX_VA_NRM in GXSetArray, instead of retaining unexplained hex literals.
naming_conventions: GX vertex attribute parameters are named attr and typed as GXAttr.
GX vertex reference/type parameter in GXSetVtxDesc is named type and documented with GX_NONE, GX_DIRECT, GX_INDEX8, and GX_INDEX16.
GXSetVtxAttrFmt uses vtxfmt for the vertex format index, count for component count, type for data type, and frac for fixed-point fractional bits.
GXSetArray uses base_ptr for the array base address and stride for byte stride.
GXSetNumTexGens uses num for the number of generated texture coordinates.
Enum typedefs in GXEnum.h can be given tags matching their typedef names, as in typedef enum GXAttr { ... } GXAttr and typedef enum GXAttrType { ... } GXAttrType.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No explicit matching tactic or reviewer matching feedback was present.
The PR appears to preserve generated code by changing names, types, comments, and enum labels while keeping the same control flow and INSERT_FIELD bit positions.
Replacing literals with enum constants is a safe readability tactic when enum values are already known and compile to the same constants.
The diff retains existing MWERKS_GEKKO assembly blocks for functions such as __GXXfVtxSpecs, __GXSetVCD, and GXSetTexCoordGen2; it does not attempt to decompile those asm blocks.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue typing GX APIs with GXAttr or GXAttrType.
Search for remaining GX functions with arg0/arg1 parameters that can be renamed from SDK documentation.
Search GXAttr.c for remaining hardcoded GX_VA numeric cases or untyped public prototypes.
Check whether later PRs rename __GXContexts.main fields touched by GXSetVtxDesc and GXSetVtxAttrFmt.
Find PRs that replace NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallbacks around MWERKS_GEKKO assembly blocks.
